message:
I've been looking into hurricane prep lately, and honestly, having a solid game plan ahead of time takes so much of the stress out of it. Ready.gov has a really straightforward resource that walks through what actually matters—supplies, evacuation routes, staying informed—without the doom-and-gloom stuff. It's kind of nice to feel prepared instead of scrambling at the last minute.

What part of storm prep do you find yourself thinking about most—the supplies, the plan, or just knowing where to get reliable info?
